step_rationale: Macrophage-driven inflammation and collagen deposition; plaque instability marker.
mechanism: Osteopontin promotes macrophage-fibroblast crosstalk and collagen remodeling, destabilizing the plaque and driving post-MI cardiac remodeling.

references.title: Serum phosphoproteome alterations associated with cardiac troponin I levels in acute myocardial infarction.

Translatome profiling reveals Itih4 as a novel smooth muscle cell-specific gene in atherosclerosis.

A combined analysis of bulk RNA-seq and scRNA-seq was performed to investigate the molecular mechanisms associated with the occurrence of myocardial infarction.
Clinical characteristics and biomarkers feature analysis using a proteomics platform in young patients with acute coronary syndrome.
Associations of osteopontin and NT-proBNP with circulating miRNA levels in acute coronary syndrome.
